page: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes hyper-threading with 2.0 threads per core, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations place the E8s_v5 predominantly in the average performance tier. The server achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU performance of 1898.68 and a multi-core score of 7757.61. Memory bandwidth is measured at 419.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 59.3 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 42.73 GB/sec for large-block writes. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1657.0 and a multi-core score of 6161.0. While database operations on Passmark score an average of 4096.64 and Redis SET operations reach 7.97 million operations per second, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 31.98 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference is also a notable bottleneck, with 7B text generation dropping to a weak 8.58 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8s_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power or local NVMe storage. The absence of local storage means users must rely on external Premium Storage, which introduces a dependency on network-attached storage performance. This server is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers such as Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded decompression or large-scale LLM inference due to its weaker performance in those specific areas.
description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the x86_64 architecture includes AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. Performance benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6161.0 and Redis SET performance reaching 7.97 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-to-large LLM text generation show weaker performance. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads like database operations, caching, and web serving, offering a balanced qualitative cost-to-memory ratio for enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe drives or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 GB of RAM, an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, and Premium Storage capability for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and dedicated Intel Xeon performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core)
Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM text generation
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
